2603.18806.title: dTRPO: Trajectory Reduction in Policy Optimization of Diffusion Large Language Models
2603.18806.paper_summary: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) introduce a new paradigm for language generation, which in turn presents new challenges for aligning them with human preferences. In this work, we aim to improve the policy optimization for dLLMs by reducing the cost of the trajectory probability calculation, thereby enabling scaled-up offline policy training. We prove that: (i) under reference policy regularization, the probability ratio of the newly unmasked tokens is an unbiased estimate of that of intermediate diffusion states, and (ii) the probability of the full trajectory can be effectively estimated with a single forward pass of a re-masked final state. By integrating these two trajectory reduction strategies into a policy optimization objective, we propose Trajectory Reduction Policy Optimization (dTRPO). We evaluate dTRPO on 7B dLLMs across instruction-following and reasoning benchmarks. Results show that it substantially improves the core performance of state-of-the-art dLLMs, achieving gains of up to 9.6% on STEM tasks, up to 4.3% on coding tasks, and up to 3.0% on instruction-following tasks. Moreover, dTRPO exhibits strong training efficiency due to its offline, single-forward nature, and achieves improved generation efficiency through high-quality outputs.
2603.18806.paper_summary_zh: 擴散大型語言模型（dLLMs）引入了一種新的語言生成範式，這反過來又為將它們與人類偏好對齊帶來了新的挑戰。在這項工作中，我們旨在通過降低軌跡概率計算的成本來改善 dLLMs 的策略優化，從而實現擴展的離線策略訓練。我們證明了：（i）在參考策略正則化下，新解掩碼標記的概率比是中間擴散狀態的無偏估計，以及（ii）完整軌跡的概率可以通過對重新掩碼的最終狀態進行單次前向傳遞來有效估計。通過將這兩種軌跡減少策略整合到策略優化目標中，我們提出了軌跡減少策略優化（dTRPO）。我們在 7B dLLMs 上對 dTRPO 進行了評估，涵蓋了遵循指令和推理基準。結果顯示，它顯著改善了最先進 dLLMs 的核心性能，在 STEM 任務上實現了高達 9.6% 的增益，在編碼任務上高達 4.3%，在遵循指令任務上高達 3.0%。此外，由於其離線、單次前向的特性，dTRPO 展現出強大的訓練效率，並通過高質量的輸出實現了改進的生成效率。
2603.18806.author: Wenxuan Zhang et.al.
2603.18806.authors: Wenxuan Zhang, Lemeng Wu, Changsheng Zhao, Ernie Chang, Mingchen Zhuge, Zechun Liu, Andy Su, Hanxian Huang, Jun Chen, Chong Zhou, Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi, Vikas Chandra, Mohamed Elhoseiny, Wei Wen
